title: cyanea copelandii subsp. haleakalaensis
intro: Cyanea copelandii subsp. haleakalaensis, also known as the Haleakala cyanea, is a rare and endangered flowering plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cyanea copelandii subsp. haleakalaensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant may involve infusions or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
